page: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The processor operates at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It also features 8639 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests, such as uncached read and write operations, also perform at average levels. For specialized tasks, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text processing and text generation for small language models, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

Given its high local storage capacity of over 8 TB and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the L12aos_v4 offers qualitative resource density for storage-heavy and memory-intensive applications. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its strong single-core execution and substantial local storage make it highly suitable for large relational databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine configured with 12 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. This instance features 8639 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1 and massive local storage, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for data-dense environments. It is well-suited for database operations, large-scale storage workloads, and memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is a storage-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and 8639 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 features 12 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and 8639 GB of local storage for database and storage workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and extensive local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and 8639 GB of local storage.
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance.
Premium Storage capable under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.
Optimized for large databases, storage-heavy applications, and memory-intensive tasks.
